# Plan Forge

> The AI-Native SDLC Forge Shop. One workshop, four stations — Smelt, Forge, Guard, Learn — covering every phase of the software lifecycle.

## What It Does

Plan Forge is an open-source framework that gives AI coding agents (Copilot, Claude, Cursor, Codex, Gemini, Windsurf, and others) a complete AI-native SDLC workshop. Instead of a single code-generation step, it runs the whole lifecycle across four stations: **Smelt** (refine idea into scope contract), **Forge** (execute slices with gates), **Guard** (LiveGuard scans secrets, drift, regression risk), and **Learn** (memory, bug registry, testbed findings feed the next plan). Drift, skipped tests, and silent architecture decisions become structurally impossible.

## Key Concepts

- **Four Stations**: Smelt → Forge → Guard → Learn. Every SDLC phase, one shop, AI-run and human-supervised.
- **AI-Native SDLC**: The lifecycle is designed for AI agents from day one — scope contracts, slice gates, quorum consensus, and post-deploy LiveGuard are all first-class citizens, not bolted on.
- **Hardened Execution Contract**: A locked-down plan with scope boundaries, validation gates, and forbidden actions that the AI must follow without deviation.
- **7-Step Pipeline** (inside the Forge station): Specify → Pre-flight → Harden → Execute → Sweep → Review → Ship
- **4 Sessions**: Session isolation prevents self-auditing. The builder and reviewer are always different AI instances.
- **Validation Gates**: Build + test must pass at every slice boundary before proceeding.
- **Scope Contract**: Defines what's in scope, out of scope, and forbidden. Drift is structurally impossible.

## Capabilities

- 65 MCP tools — core orchestration + LiveGuard + Watcher + Crucible + Tempering + Bug Registry + Testbed. Start with `forge_capabilities` for full discovery; call `mcp-call <tool>` from the CLI for any tool without a dedicated wrapper.
- 45+ CLI commands (`pforge <command>`) — plan hardening (run-plan, analyze, sweep), LiveGuard (drift, incident, secret-scan, env-diff, dep-watch, hotspot, regression-guard, fix-proposal, quorum-analyze, health-trend, deploy-log), releases (self-update, version-bump, migrate-memory), testbed (testbed-happypath), introspection (smith, check, status, tour), agent setup (claude, codex, cursor, copilot), org-rules export.
- LiveGuard tools — post-coding intelligence: drift scoring, incident tracking, secret scanning, dependency vulnerability monitoring, regression guard, health trend with Health DNA fingerprint, alert triage, environment diff, fix proposals with code snippets, quorum analysis, composite health check (`forge_liveguard_run`).
- Crucible + Tempering — community forge intake, lifecycle state machine, and temper-quality scoring for community extensions.
- Bug Registry — native bug tracking (register/list/link/update/close/stats) integrated into the dashboard timeline + notifications.
- Testbed — happy-path scenario runner with Chapter 8 hooks for release validation.
- Forge Intelligence — self-recursive improvement: auto-tune escalation chains from model performance, cost estimate calibration from historical actuals, adaptive quorum thresholds, slice auto-split advisories.
- LiveGuard Intelligence — recurring incident detection (auto-escalate systemic), fix proposal outcome tracking, hotspot test priority, Project Health DNA for decay detection.
- OpenBrain integration — auto-capture findings to persistent memory; pipeline prompts search memory before acting.
- 19 reviewer agents (6 stack-specific + 8 cross-stack + 5 pipeline)
- 13 slash command skills
- 15-18 auto-loading instruction files per preset
- 15 scaffolding prompt templates per app preset
- Live dashboard at localhost:3100/dashboard — multi-tab (FORGE, LIVEGUARD, CRUCIBLE, BUG REGISTRY, NOTIFICATIONS, TIMELINE/SEARCH, WATCHER)
- Quorum mode: multi-model consensus for complex slices (--quorum=power, --quorum=speed)
- Auto-escalation: automatic model promotion on slice failure (self-tuning from history)
- Agent-per-slice routing: assign different models per role
- Cost tracking with multi-model pricing table (self-calibrating estimates)
- OTLP-compatible trace generation
- LiveGuard telemetry: per-tool JSONL event log + WebSocket hub broadcast
- Auto-regenerated discovery: `pforge-mcp/tools.json` + `pforge-mcp/cli-schema.json` rewritten on every server start from `capabilities.mjs` (single source of truth)

## Supported Stacks (9 Presets)

dotnet, typescript, python, java, go, swift, rust, php, azure-iac

## Supported AI Tools

GitHub Copilot (primary), Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Generic (any AI tool)

## Entry Points

- AI agents: Read AGENT-SETUP.md
- Humans: Read docs/QUICKSTART-WALKTHROUGH.md
- MCP clients: Call forge_capabilities
- Machine-readable: .well-known/plan-forge.json

## Quick Start

```
git clone https://github.com/srnichols/plan-forge.git my-project-plans
cd my-project-plans
.\setup.ps1 -Preset <stack>
```

## REST API (MCP Server — localhost:3100)

The MCP server exposes a REST API for external integrations (OpenClaw, CI pipelines, webhooks).
All authenticated endpoints use: `Authorization: Bearer <bridge.approvalSecret>` or `?token=<secret>`.

### Discovery
- `GET /.well-known/plan-forge.json` — full machine-readable capability surface
- `GET /api/capabilities` — same as forge_capabilities MCP tool

### Runs
- `GET /api/status` — current run status
- `GET /api/runs` — run history
- `POST /api/runs/trigger` — start a plan run remotely (auth required). Body: `{ plan, quorum?, model? }`
- `POST /api/runs/abort` — abort in-progress run (auth required)

### Memory (OpenBrain)
- `GET /api/memory` — OpenBrain connection status
- `POST /api/memory/search` — search project memory. Body: `{ query, project?, limit? }`
- `POST /api/memory/capture` — capture a thought (auth required). Body: `{ content, type?, source?, created_by? }`

### Bridge (Notifications + Approvals)
- `GET /api/bridge/status` — channel status + pending approvals
- `POST /api/bridge/approve/:runId` — approve/reject a run (auth required). Body: `{ action: "approve"|"reject" }`
- `GET /api/bridge/approve/:runId` — browser-friendly approval link (Telegram buttons). Query: `?action=approve|reject`

### Other
- `GET /api/cost` — cost report
- `GET /api/workers` — detected CLI workers
- `GET /api/plans` — available plan files

## Related Projects

- OpenBrain (persistent semantic memory): https://github.com/srnichols/OpenBrain
- OpenClaw (always-on AI gateway): https://github.com/srnichols/OpenClaw
